overview.short: Gowalkot is a small fort located on the southern bank of Vashishti River, about 10.0 km (6.2 miles) from Chiplun in Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Gowalkot is a small fort located on the southern bank of Vashishti River, about 10.0 km (6.2 miles) from Chiplun in Maharashtra, India. This fort is guarded by the river on three sides and a trench on the fourth side. Its population which includes Hindus, Muslims, and Buddhists. According to old natives of Gowalkot, the history of Gowalkot goes back to a King, who was Hindu and finally was converted in Muslim, his surname was "Chougle", and most of the land in Gowalkot is owned by the Chougle family.

topPlaces.name: Dhamandevi
topPlaces.description: Dhamandevi is a village in Khed Tehsil, Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra, India. Its population is 2,758.

topPlaces.name: Chiplun
topPlaces.description: Chiplun ( [t͡ʃipɭuːɳ]) is a city in Ratnagiri district in the state of Maharashtra, India. It is one of the financial and commercial hubs of Ratnagiri district, and the headquarters of Chiplun taluka.

topPlaces.name: Mirjoli
topPlaces.description: Mirjoli is a small village on the banks of the Vashishti River in the mandal of Chiplun, Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Kherdi
topPlaces.description: Kherdi is a census town in Ratnagiri in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It has Maharashtra Industrial Development Corporation (MIDC).

topPlaces.name: Asurde
topPlaces.description: Asurde is a village located in Chiplun sub-district, Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra, India. As of the 2011 Indian Census, it contained 558 households and had a population of 2335 people.

topPlaces.name: Bahirwali
topPlaces.description: Baharwali is the largest village in the taluka of Khed, India. Located about 28 km (17.40 mi) from the town of Khed, Ratnagiri, Baharwali is situated near the banks of the Jagbudi River and Vashishti River.

topPlaces.name: Bhawani Waghjai Temple, Terav
topPlaces.description: The Shree Bhawani Waghjai Temple, Terav is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddesses Bhawani and Waghjai. It is located in village Terav, Taluka Chiplun, Dist.

items.note: Dhamandevi is a village in Khed Tehsil, Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra, India. Its population is 2,758.

Chiplun ( [t͡ʃipɭuːɳ]) is a city in Ratnagiri district in the state of Maharashtra, India. It is one of the financial and commercial hubs of Ratnagiri district, and the headquarters of Chiplun taluka.
Mirjoli is a small village on the banks of the Vashishti River in the mandal of Chiplun, Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra.
Kherdi is a census town in Ratnagiri in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It has Maharashtra Industrial Development Corporation (MIDC).
Asurde is a village located in Chiplun sub-district, Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra, India. As of the 2011 Indian Census, it contained 558 households and had a population of 2335 people.

Baharwali is the largest village in the taluka of Khed, India. Located about 28 km (17.40 mi) from the town of Khed, Ratnagiri, Baharwali is situated near the banks of the Jagbudi River and Vashishti River.
The Shree Bhawani Waghjai Temple, Terav is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddesses Bhawani and Waghjai. It is located in village Terav, Taluka Chiplun, Dist.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gowalkot?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gowalkot is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (124 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (117 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gowalkot?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gowalkot at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gowalkot?
faq.a: Stays in Gowalkot start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gowalkot?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dhamandevi, Chiplun, Mirjoli, Kherdi, Asurde.
